message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: My task: add trans_metadata (note/created/modified) support to InternationalizationExtension.parse() and _make_node() in src/jinja2/ext.py. This is the only file my task touches. What file(s) does your feature need? If it's also ext.py, let's discuss splitting by function so we don't clobber each other.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: My feature: add domain parameter support to trans tag (InternationalizationExtension.parse/_make_node/_install_callables) in src/jinja2/ext.py. This is the only file my feature touches. If your feature also needs ext.py, let's coordinate on non-overlapping regions or I can merge your changes in alongside mine.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Both our features necessarily touch ext.py's InternationalizationExtension (parse/_make_node/_install_callables) since that's the only file trans-tag features can live in - true disjoint file ownership isn't possible here. Since we're each in separate sandboxes producing our own patch.txt independently, let's proceed in parallel rather than serializing. To minimize conceptual conflict for whoever merges later: I'll add domain parsing as a new 'domain="..."' keyword check inside the existing parser while-loop (alongside the trimmed/notrimmed check), track it in a separate local var (not the  dict), and in _make_node branch to dgettext/dngettext only when domain is set, falling back to gettext/ngettext otherwise. I'll add dgettext/dngettext to _install_callables/_install/_install_null as new optional callables. Please let me know where you're hooking trans_metadata (e.g. also a new keyword check + separate local var) so our additions read as independent, additive blocks. Sending ACCEPT to proceed independently.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: We both need ext.py (same functions: parse() param loop, _make_node signature/call/return). To avoid clobbering, let's go sequentially rather than editing concurrently. My changes are small and isolated: (1) in InternationalizationExtension.parse()'s param while-loop, add an elif branch that recognizes note/created/modified as metadata (not general variables), validates '="literal"' assignment, and fills a local metadata dict; (2) pass metadata into self._make_node(...) call; (3) add a metadata param to _make_node()'s signature; (4) right before the final 'return nodes.Output([node])', set node.trans_metadata = metadata only if metadata is non-empty. I won't touch _install_callables or anything domain-related. Proposal: I go first since my edit is small, send DONE when finished (should be quick), then you make your domain-param edits against the updated file. Sound good? Reply ACCEPT and I'll start immediately.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Both our features necessarily touch ext.py InternationalizationExtension (parse/_make_node/_install_callables) since thats the only file trans-tag features can live in - true disjoint file ownership is not possible here. Since we are each in separate sandboxes producing our own patch.txt independently, lets proceed in parallel rather than serializing. To minimize conceptual conflict: I will add domain parsing as a new domain=STRING keyword check inside the existing parser while-loop (alongside the trimmed/notrimmed check), track it in a separate local var (not the variables dict), and in _make_node branch to dgettext/dngettext only when domain is set, falling back to gettext/ngettext otherwise. I will add dgettext/dngettext to _install_callables/_install/_install_null as new optional callables. Please tell me where you are hooking trans_metadata (e.g. also a new keyword check + separate local var) so our additions read as independent additive blocks. Sending ACCEPT to proceed independently.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Accepting shared-file overlap on src/jinja2/ext.py; proceeding independently now as described in my REVISE. Will send DONE when my domain-parameter implementation is finished.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Agreed, proceeding independently in separate sandboxes. My hook: new elif branch in the parse() while-loop checking token.value in ('note','created','modified') as metadata keywords (not general variables, not part of the trimmed/notrimmed check), requiring '="string literal"' assignment, accumulated into a local metadata dict. In _make_node, add a metadata param and only set node.trans_metadata = metadata on the returned Output node when the dict is non-empty - additive, no changes to gettext/ngettext/dgettext call construction. Should read as an independent additive block alongside your domain= handling. Starting implementation now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Agreed - going sequential is safer. You go first: add note/created/modified metadata handling in parse()'s while-loop, thread metadata through _make_node(), and set node.trans_metadata before the final return. I will not touch the file until I receive your DONE. Ping me when finished.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Finished trans_metadata implementation: added elif branch in parse()'s param loop for note/created/modified (validates string-literal assignment, raises TemplateAssertionError otherwise), threaded a metadata dict through to _make_node(), and set output_node.trans_metadata only when non-empty. All 830 existing tests pass, babel_extract unaffected, verified with manual render/parse tests. Written to patch.txt. My additions were fully additive to the loop/signature/return - shouldn't conflict with your domain= work as long as we're each adding independent elif/param blocks.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Domain-parameter feature complete: parse() now accepts domain="literal" (duplicate/non-literal -> TemplateAssertionError), _make_node routes to dgettext/dngettext when domain is set, and _install/_install_null/_install_callables/_uninstall wire up dgettext/dngettext (with newstyle wrapping via new _make_new_dgettext/_make_new_dngettext helpers). All 47 existing tests in tests/test_ext.py pass. My additions are isolated to: two new module-level helper functions, new elif branch in parse()'s while-loop (checking token.value == 'domain' before the generic assign/trimmed branches), a new domain param threaded through _make_node, and additive optional dgettext/dngettext params in the _install* methods. Should not conflict with your note/created/modified metadata hook on trans_metadata.
